title: LaplacianRecursion
summary: Formalizes the iterative ambient-Laplacian recursion corresponding to Proposition 3.7 in the PE renormalized-integral reference.

mathematicalContent.heading: Mathematical role
mathematicalContent.body: Formalizes the iterative ambient-Laplacian recursion corresponding to Proposition 3.7 in the PE renormalized-integral reference.

mathematicalContent.heading: How to read the translation
mathematicalContent.body: The correspondence below follows Ambient/PE/Invariants/LaplacianRecursion.lean declaration by declaration. A definition is translated by its defining expression, a theorem by its quantified proposition, and a structure, class, or inductive type by the mathematical data encoded in its fields or constructors. The original Lean signature remains visible beside every translation.

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Coefficient 4 (k+j) (n-2k-2j-1) / n in the j-th Case Laplacian factor. The signed numerator is formed in Int before coercion to Rat. In explicit terms, for n, k, j in the natural numbers, its defining expression is 4 : Rat times k plus j times n : Int minus 2 times k : Int minus 2 times j : Int minus 1 : Int : Rat divided by n : Rat.
correspondence.leanType: def caseLaplacianCoefficient (n k j : Nat) : Rat

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: One factor in Equation (3.1). In explicit terms, for n, k, j in the natural numbers, and i of type scalar invariant, its defining expression is ⟨ expr : equals Tensor Invariant Expr.add Tensor Invariant Expr.ambient Laplacian I.1.expr Tensor Invariant Expr.scalar Mul -case Laplacian Coefficient n k j Tensor Invariant Expr.j Mul I.1.expr rank : equals 0 weight : equals I.1.weight minus 2 parity : equals I.1.parity, rfl ⟩.
correspondence.mathematicalFormula: \operatorname{CaseLaplacianFactor}(n,k,j,I)=⟨ { \operatorname{expr} = \operatorname{TensorInvariantExpr}.\operatorname{add} (\operatorname{TensorInvariantExpr}.\operatorname{laplacian} I.\operatorname{1}.\operatorname{expr}) (\operatorname{TensorInvariantExpr}.\operatorname{scalarMul} (-\operatorname{caseLaplacianCoefficient} n k j) (\operatorname{TensorInvariantExpr}.\operatorname{jMul} I.\operatorname{1}.\operatorname{expr})) \operatorname{rank} = 0 \operatorname{weight} = I.\operatorname{1}.\operatorname{weight} - 2 \operatorname{parity} = I.\operatorname{1}.\operatorname{parity} }, \operatorname{rfl} ⟩
correspondence.leanType: def CaseLaplacianFactor (n k j : Nat) (I : ScalarInvariant) : ScalarInvariant

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: For n, k, j in the natural numbers, and I of type Scalar Invariant, Case Laplacian Factor n k j I.1.weight equals I.1.weight minus 2.
correspondence.leanType: theorem CaseLaplacianFactor_weight (n k j : Nat) (I : ScalarInvariant) : (CaseLaplacianFactor n k j I).1.weight = I.1.weight - 2

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Iteration of Equation (3.1), ordered by increasing j. In explicit terms, for n, k in the natural numbers, its defining expression is | 0, I maps to I | ell plus 1, I maps to Case Laplacian Factor n k ell Case Laplacian Recursion n k ell I.
correspondence.mathematicalFormula: \begin{aligned}\operatorname{CaseLaplacianRecursion}(0, I)&=I\\\operatorname{CaseLaplacianRecursion}(\operatorname{ell} + 1, I)&=\operatorname{CaseLaplacianFactor} n k \operatorname{ell} (\operatorname{CaseLaplacianRecursion} n k \operatorname{ell} I)\end{aligned}
correspondence.leanType: def CaseLaplacianRecursion (n k : Nat) : Nat -> ScalarInvariant -> ScalarInvariant

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: For n, k in the natural numbers, and I of type Scalar Invariant, Case Laplacian Recursion n k 0 I equals I.
correspondence.leanType: theorem CaseLaplacianRecursion_zero (n k : Nat) (I : ScalarInvariant) : CaseLaplacianRecursion n k 0 I = I

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: For n, k, ell in the natural numbers, and I of type Scalar Invariant, Case Laplacian Recursion n k ell plus 1 I equals Case Laplacian Factor n k ell Case Laplacian Recursion n k ell I.
correspondence.mathematicalFormula: \forall\,n,k,ell\colon \mathbb{N},\;I\colon \operatorname{ScalarInvariant},\quad \operatorname{CaseLaplacianRecursion} n k (\operatorname{ell} + 1) I = \operatorname{CaseLaplacianFactor} n k \operatorname{ell} (\operatorname{CaseLaplacianRecursion} n k \operatorname{ell} I)
correspondence.leanType: theorem CaseLaplacianRecursion_succ (n k ell : Nat) (I : ScalarInvariant) : CaseLaplacianRecursion n k (ell + 1) I = CaseLaplacianFactor n k ell (CaseLaplacianRecursion n k ell I)

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Every recursion step lowers weight by two. In symbols, the proposition is the statement displayed below.
correspondence.leanType: theorem CaseLaplacianRecursion_weight (n k ell : Nat) (I : ScalarInvariant) : (CaseLaplacianRecursion n k ell I).1.weight = I.1.weight - 2 * (ell : Int)

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Iterated ambient Laplacian. In explicit terms, its defining expression is | 0, I maps to I | ell plus 1, I maps to Ambient Scalar Invariant.ambient Laplacian ambient Laplacian Iter ell I.
For I of type Ambient Scalar Invariant, ambient Laplacian Iter 0 I equals I.
correspondence.leanType: theorem ambientLaplacianIter_zero (I : AmbientScalarInvariant) : ambientLaplacianIter 0 I = I

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: For ell in the natural numbers, and I of type Ambient Scalar Invariant, ambient Laplacian Iter ell plus 1 I equals Ambient Scalar Invariant.ambient Laplacian ambient Laplacian Iter ell I.
correspondence.leanType: theorem ambientLaplacianIter_succ (ell : Nat) (I : AmbientScalarInvariant) : ambientLaplacianIter (ell + 1) I = AmbientScalarInvariant.laplacian (ambientLaplacianIter ell I)

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: For ell in the natural numbers, and I of type Ambient Scalar Invariant, ambient Laplacian Iter ell I.1.weight equals I.1.weight minus 2 times ell : Int.
correspondence.leanType: theorem ambientLaplacianIter_weight (ell : Nat) (I : AmbientScalarInvariant) : (ambientLaplacianIter ell I).1.weight = I.1.weight - 2 * (ell : Int)

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Semantic form of the weighted-pullback ambient Laplacian identity used in Proposition 3.7. Its mathematical data are: associated step: for every n k j I : Scalar Invariant Itilde : Ambient Scalar Invariant, Scalar Straightenable I Itilde maps to I.1.weight equals -2 times k plus j : Int maps to Invariant Evaluation Model.Associated Case Laplacian Factor n k j I.1 Ambient Scalar Invariant.ambient Laplacian Itilde.1; critical slice conformal: for every n Itilde : Ambient Scalar Invariant, Itilde.1.weight equals - n : Int maps to Is Scalar Conformal Invariant restrict To Einstein Slice Itilde.
correspondence.mathematicalFormula: \begin{aligned}\operatorname{associated\_step}&\colon \forall n k j (I \colon \operatorname{ScalarInvariant}) (\operatorname{Itilde} \colon \operatorname{AmbientScalarInvariant}), \operatorname{ScalarStraightenable} I \operatorname{Itilde} \longrightarrow I.\operatorname{1}.\operatorname{weight} = -2 \cdot (k + j \colon \mathbb{Z}) \longrightarrow \operatorname{InvariantEvaluationModel}.\operatorname{Associated} (\operatorname{CaseLaplacianFactor} n k j I).1 (\operatorname{AmbientScalarInvariant}.\operatorname{laplacian} \operatorname{Itilde}).1\\\operatorname{critical\_slice\_conformal}&\colon \forall n (\operatorname{Itilde} \colon \operatorname{AmbientScalarInvariant}), \operatorname{Itilde}.\operatorname{1}.\operatorname{weight} = -(n \colon \mathbb{Z}) \longrightarrow \operatorname{IsScalarConformalInvariant} (\operatorname{restrictToEinsteinSlice} \operatorname{Itilde})\end{aligned}

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: One application of the ambient Laplacian preserves association. In symbols, the proposition is the statement displayed below.
correspondence.mathematicalFormula: \forall\,\operatorname{InvariantEvaluationModel},\;\operatorname{EinsteinAmbientLaplacianFormula},\;n,k,j\colon \mathbb{N},\;I\colon \operatorname{ScalarInvariant},\;Itilde\colon \operatorname{AmbientScalarInvariant},\;hStraight\colon \operatorname{ScalarStraightenable} I \operatorname{Itilde},\;hWeight\colon I.\operatorname{1}.\operatorname{weight} = -2 \cdot (k + j \colon \mathbb{Z}),\quad \operatorname{ScalarStraightenable} (\operatorname{CaseLaplacianFactor} n k j I) (\operatorname{AmbientScalarInvariant}.\operatorname{laplacian} \operatorname{Itilde})
correspondence.leanType: theorem straightenable_laplacian_step [InvariantEvaluationModel] [EinsteinAmbientLaplacianFormula] (n k j : Nat) (I : ScalarInvariant) (Itilde : AmbientScalarInvariant) (hStraight : ScalarStraightenable I Itilde) (hWeight : I.1.weight = -2 * ((k + j : Nat) : Int)) : ScalarStraightenable (CaseLaplacianFactor n k j I) (AmbientScalarInvariant.laplacian Itilde)

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Proposition 3.7: the recursive base invariant is associated to the iterated ambient Laplacian. In symbols, the proposition is the statement displayed below.
correspondence.leanType: theorem straightenable_laplacian_iterate [InvariantEvaluationModel] [EinsteinAmbientLaplacianFormula] (n k ell : Nat) (I : ScalarInvariant) (Itilde : AmbientScalarInvariant) (hStraight : ScalarStraightenable I Itilde) (hWeight : I.1.weight = -2 * (k : Int)) : ScalarStraightenable (CaseLaplacianRecursion n k ell I) (ambientLaplacianIter ell Itilde)

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: At critical weight, restriction of the ambient iterate to the Einstein slice is a scalar conformal invariant. In symbols, the proposition is the statement displayed below.
correspondence.leanType: theorem ambient_laplacian_iter_einstein_slice_conformal [InvariantEvaluationModel] [EinsteinAmbientLaplacianFormula] (n ell : Nat) (Itilde : AmbientScalarInvariant) (hCritical : Itilde.1.weight - 2 * (ell : Int) = -(n : Int)) : IsScalarConformalInvariant (restrictToEinsteinSlice (ambientLaplacianIter ell Itilde))

references.locator: Proposition 3.7, Proposition 3.10, Lemma 4.1, and Theorems 1.1/1.4 as applicable
references.note: Primary source for the renormalized-curvature-integral mechanism represented by these modules.

limitations: The displayed Lean results are checked relative to structures, typeclasses, certificates, or model laws supplied as hypotheses. The module does not by itself derive every interface field from a concrete geometric backend.
